method: ALIGNN jv_supercon_a2F_alignn predicts alpha2F(omega) on 100-bin 0-100 meV grid (JARVIS-SuperconDB spec); integrated to lambda = 2*sum(a2F/w)*dw and wlog = exp((2/lambda)*sum(a2F*ln(w)/w)*dw); Allen-Dynes Tc with mu*=0.1.
grid_spec: JARVIS-SuperconDB: alpha2F from 0 to 100 meV, 100 points; columns a2F/wlog/lamb (figshare 21370572). 1 meV = 11.604 K.
key_diagnosis: The failure is localized to lambda (electron-phonon coupling), NOT omega_log. For hydrides the model predicts lambda 0.34-0.48 when the true high-P value is ~2.0-2.5 (a 4-5x underprediction). omega_log is roughly correct (H3S 216K, LaH10 223K, YH6 253K — high, consistent with H-dominated phonons). This is the precise numerical fingerprint of the ambient training distribution: the JARVIS-SuperconDB never contained lambda~2 coupling, so the GNN cannot extrapolate to it.
cross_path_synthesis: Three independent ALIGNN/BETE-NET routes (end-to-end BETE-NET Tc; end-to-end jv_supercon_tc_alignn; a2F-decomposed -> Allen-Dynes) ALL fail on high-P hydrides, all via the same lambda-underprediction mechanism. There is NO distinct public hydride-trained model (figshare 21370572 is the dataset, baked into the same jv_supercon models). Ambient-ML path is exhausted.
forward_path: Direct DFT electron-phonon (Quantum ESPRESSO ph.x) computes lambda and alpha2F from first principles at the actual high-P lattice — pressure-aware by construction, no training-distribution dependence. This is the only route that can recover hydride lambda~2. Pool:ubu-1 (12 cores, 30GB, sudo passwordless, QE installable via apt) is the execution host. H3S (Im-3m, 4 atoms/cell, ~150 GPa) is the tractable validation case.
r4_invariant: absorbed=false · gate_type=simulation-only-prediction · domain=material. Pattern 1+2 무손상. Null result REFINES path to first-principles DFT — goal not abandoned.
citations: arxiv:2312.12694 Wines & Choudhary, Materials Futures 2024 (hydride GNN = ALIGNN on JARVIS, not a distinct model)
